summary: Add the wiims command for GET /api/v1/wiims (Why Is It Moving), unwrapping the {"wiims":[...]} envelope.
reason: WIIM (Why Is It Moving) is a live Benzinga endpoint (/api/v1/wiims) that the ecosystem research called out as a coverage gap, but it is absent from the official OpenAPI specs bundled in the run, so generation could not emit it. The endpoint wraps its list in a single {"wiims":[...]} key, which the command unwraps so count/table/JSON output match sibling list commands. A regen must re-add this hand-mapped endpoint and keep the envelope unwrap.
validated_outcome: Live GET /api/v1/wiims returns 200; --json output shows the unwrapped item array with description/security fields; go build/vet pass.

deferred_to_upstream.feature: WIIM (/api/v1/wiims) is not in Benzinga's published OpenAPI specs; the path and params were reverse-engineered against the live API.
deferred_to_upstream.reason: If Benzinga publishes a spec for WIIM (or the generator learns the news-api/wiims surface), this hand-mapped command should be superseded by the generated one.
